reason: All tier-A rubric failures are intrinsic test-design problems, not instruction problems: (1) behavior_in_task_description — tests assert accuracy_threshold==0.69 exactly; the instruction cannot state this literal without violating no_solution_leakage, since 0.69 IS the gold fix's change. (2) solution_uniqueness_guard — tests reject alternative valid thresholds (e.g. 0.68 or 0.65 with assertGreaterEqual) that would solve the flaky CI problem identically. (3) anti_cheating_measures — tests embed exact values ('0.69', 'assertGreaterEqual') as string literals in the oracle; no instruction can prevent an agent from grepping /tests/test_outputs.py. The instruction passes no_solution_leakage and instruction_no_hint_leakage. NOP=0 and GOLD=1 confirmed. The tier-C no_typos failure was a false positive (judge's reasoning contradicted itself).
